name: Wildebeest Chess
author: R. Wayne Schmittberger (1987) / AGP port
description: Wildebeest Chess (R. Wayne Schmittberger, 1987) on an 11x10 board (11 files, 10 ranks). Adds two leapers per side: the Camel, a (1,3) leaper (an elongated, colour-bound knight), and the Wildebeest, which moves as either a knight or a camel. Each side has 1 King, 1 Queen, 1 Wildebeest, 2 Rooks, 2 Knights, 2 Camels, 2 Bishops and 11 Pawns. Pawns advance any distance while staying in their own half, capture diagonally, and have multi-square en passant; the king castles a variable 1-4 squares toward a rook; pawns promote only to Queen or Wildebeest. Checkmate wins; stalemate draws by default, with an option for the authentic rule that stalemate also wins.
